pages.name: How the Maps Ship (and How to Dress Them)

text.content:
Ashen now ships a complete, original, grid-aligned battlemap for every scene, rendered procedurally just for this module — correct size, square 5-ft grid, mood lighting, with cover, bonfires, fog gates and charge lanes placed where the encounter needs them. They contain no FromSoftware art and no baked grid lines (Foundry draws the grid on top), so they're copyright-safe and align perfectly. Every scene also carries a one-paragraph layout brief on its flags.ashen.brief, summarised on the next page.

Zero setup

The maps are already wired in as each scene's background (they live in the module at modules/ashen-of-lothric/maps/). Import the module, open a scene, and the map is just there — token vision and fog on, squares at 5 ft. You can run the entire one-shot on the shipped art with nothing else to do.

Want fancier art? (optional)

  • AI-paint your own — copy the per-arena prompts on the next page into an image generator, then swap the file (below). Best balance of effort vs. looks.
  • Drop in a free / CC battlemap — curated sources two pages on.

Swapping a map (2 minutes)

  1. Get a map image. Note its pixel size.
  2. Either replace the file at modules/ashen-of-lothric/maps/<scene>.webp (keeps it auto-wired), or open the scene → Configure (gear) → Background and point it at your new image.
  3. On the Dimensions tab, match Width/Height to the image pixels (shipped sizes are on the next page), then use the grid-alignment tool so one square = one map square. Keep Grid Distance = 5 ft.
  4. Save & re-place tokens.

Tip: keep the same pixel dimensions as the shipped map and the grid already lines up — no realignment needed.

Each scene ships at the size below (square 5-ft grid) and the rendered map is already wired in. If you swap in your own art, match these square counts / pixels for a clean, no-realign fit.

SceneSquaresPixelsThe space
0 · Region Overviewgridless3200×2400Between-scene map; show the dragon in sight, orient backtracking.
1 · Cemetery / Gundyr30×243000×2400Grave slope funnels into a circular arena; pillars for cover on approach.
2 · Firelink Hub28×222800×2200Safe hub; Fire Keeper, Handmaid, Andre, Sword Master, central bonfire.
3 · High Wall / Courtyard36×283600×2800Entry hall → farm courtyard; hollows, archers on ramparts, hound pack.
4 · Dragon Bridge40×204000×2000Long exposed span; mark fire lanes, cover, and the dead zone under the perch.
5 · Outrider Arena26×262600×2600Tight enclosed duel; 1–2 pillars to break charge lanes, nowhere to turtle.
6 · Pus Wall & Shortcut30×223000×2200Rampart walk with ledges; corpse erupts; a kicked ladder opens the shortcut.
7 · Vordt Arena30×303000×3000Fog gate → long frozen hall; room to run the Frost Charge; victory bonfire beyond.

The full encounter detail (enemy placement, cover, the three Dragon solutions, the Teaching-Death beats) lives in the DM Codex journal and on each scene's brief flag.

Prefer painted art? Paste any prompt below into an image generator (ChatGPT / DALL·E, Midjourney, etc.), then swap the result in using the steps on the “How the Maps Ship” page. Each prompt is tuned to the same layout the shipped map uses, so enemy placement and the DM Codex notes still line up.

How to get a usable result

  • Match the shape. Each prompt ends with an aspect ratio matching that scene's square count — keep it so the map fills the grid without stretching.
  • Top-down only. If the AI returns an angled / 3-D view, add “strict top-down orthographic view, no perspective.”
  • No baked grid. Foundry draws the grid; ask for “no grid lines.” After import use the grid-alignment tool.
  • Keep it under 2 MB. Export WebP or PNG and downscale to roughly the pixel size on the previous page.
  • Stay original. Keep it “dark-fantasy, inspired by” — don't ask it to copy Dark Souls screenshots or FromSoftware art.

1 · Cemetery of Ash — Gundyr (5:4)

Top-down orthographic tabletop RPG battlemap, dark fantasy, no grid lines. A misty ash-grey cemetery on a gentle slope of broken, leaning gravestones that funnels downward into a wide circular dueling arena of cracked flagstone. Ruined stone pillars and piles of rubble along the upper approach provide cover; the central arena floor is open and clear. A small ring of ash holding an upright coiled sword (an unlit bonfire) at the far lower edge. Desaturated, grim, foggy, cold light. No text or labels. 5:4 aspect ratio.

2 · Firelink Shrine — Hub (5:4)

Top-down orthographic dark-fantasy battlemap, no grid lines. A ruined sanctuary interior: a roughly circular stone hall with a warm glowing fire (a bonfire) at the centre and five alcoves around the ring — a raised throne nook, a shadowed merchant alcove, a blacksmith's forge glowing orange, a stair landing, and a quiet resting nook. Cracked flagstone, broken arches, firelight pooling at centre, cold shadow at the edges. Calm safe-haven mood, no enemies. No text. 5:4 aspect ratio.

3 · High Wall of Lothric — Courtyard (9:7)

Top-down orthographic dark-fantasy battlemap, no grid lines. A castle gatehouse hall opening through a broken portcullis into a wide ruined courtyard. Raised rampart walkways with archer perches at the four corners; scattered cover of broken pillars, rubble, and tumbled gravestones; grey weather-worn stone under an overcast sky. Open enough for a skirmish of a dozen figures. Grim and windswept. No text. 9:7 aspect ratio.

4 · Dragon Bridge (2:1)

Top-down orthographic dark-fantasy battlemap, no grid lines. A long narrow stone bridge spanning a black void, raked by dragonfire. The open central span is scorched black (a clear danger lane); stone archways and fallen rubble line the sides as cover; a wide perch platform at one end glows with heat where a dragon roosts. A few charred corpses lie on the span. Sheer drops along both long edges. Smoky, grim, embers in the air. No text. 2:1 aspect ratio.

5 · Outrider Knight — Arena (1:1)

Top-down orthographic dark-fantasy battlemap, no grid lines. A tight, fully enclosed square stone chamber built for a brutal duel: high windowless walls, no visible exits, frost-rimed flagstone, one or two broken pillars breaking up the floor. Claustrophobic, cold, nowhere to hide. Faint blue light. No text. Square 1:1 aspect ratio.

6 · Pus-of-Man Wall & Shortcut (4:3)

Top-down orthographic dark-fantasy battlemap, no grid lines. A narrow castle rampart walkway along the top of a high wall: stone battlements (merlons) line one long side, a sheer drop runs along the other. A slumped, swollen corpse lies mid-walk. Scattered rubble for cover. A kicked-down wooden ladder at the far end opens a shortcut downward. Sickly grey-green pallor, overcast, grim. No text. 4:3 aspect ratio.

7 · Vordt of the Boreal Valley (1:1)

Top-down orthographic dark-fantasy battlemap, no grid lines. A long frozen cathedral hall: a narrow icy approach at the top passes through a shimmering pale fog gate into a vast hall of frost-covered flagstone, with a faint wide charge lane down the centre, patches of slick blue ice, and broken pillars along the sides. A glowing bonfire just before the entrance and another past the far doorway. Deep blue cold light, drifting frost and mist. No text. Square 1:1 aspect ratio.

0 · Region Overview (4:3)

An illustrated dark-fantasy region map, top-down, parchment-and-shadow style, no grid lines. Seven labelled locations linked by a winding road: a cemetery (lower left), then a firelit shrine hub, a high castle wall, a fire-raked bridge with a distant dragon, a knight's arena, a rampart walk, and a frozen boss hall (lower right). Include a branching fork up to the bridge and a faint dashed shortcut looping back to the shrine. Muted gold route on dark slate, drifting fog, moody and hand-drawn. 4:3 aspect ratio.

Hand-painting instead? The same per-arena briefs drive the free/CC search on the next page — look for “circular arena,” “long bridge,” “frozen hall,” etc.

A curated starting list for grim, ruined, snowy, castle-rampart maps and dark-fantasy tokens. Always check the individual asset's licence before using it — terms vary by creator, and some free tiers are personal-use or CC-BY-NC (fine for a private, non-monetised game; credit where asked).

Battlemaps

  • Dyson Logos (dysonlogos.blog) — huge free dungeon/keep library, commercial-friendly with credit. Great for ruined halls and ramparts.
  • 2-Minute Tabletop — free + paid maps and map-making assets; dark-fantasy and castle sets.
  • Forgotten Adventures — enormous free map & asset library (CC-BY-NC); excellent grim/ruined/snow tilesets and pre-made maps.
  • Cartography Assets (cartographyassets.com) — filter by free; many CC maps and brushes.
  • r/battlemaps & r/dndmaps — lots of free maps; read each poster's usage note.
  • Czepeku (free samples) & Tom Cartos — cinematic maps incl. snowy keeps and bridges (mostly paid; some free).

Tokens & portraits

  • Forgotten Adventures Tokens — thousands of free top-down tokens (undead, knights, beasts) fitting the hollow/knight roster.
  • Game-icons.net (CC-BY 3.0) — the same icon set Foundry uses; perfect for item cards, abilities, and minimalist tokens.
  • Foundry core art (the icons/ paths already used throughout this module) — ships with Foundry, safe to use.
  • Token Stamp 2 (rolladvantage) — free tool to frame any image into a round token.

Make your own (optional)

  • Dungeondraft (paid, one-time) — quick custom battlemaps; pairs well with Forgotten Adventures asset packs.
  • Inkarnate / Wonderdraft — region/overview maps for Scene 0.

None of these are FromSoftware assets. Use them to evoke Lothric's mood without shipping copyrighted art in the module.
